title: One of the most scenic drives in America is in New Hampshire
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Kancamagus Highway in New Hampshire ranked No. 5 in USA Today's 2026 10Best Readers’ Choice Awards for scenic drives.
The Kancamagus Highway offers over 30 miles of stunning White Mountains views and is famous for fall foliage.
USA Today readers voted from nominees selected by travel experts to determine the top 10 scenic drives in America.

title: A Kennedy-listed Hyannis ‘dream’ home with private beach access — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: The Kennedy-listed Hyannis home features 5 bedrooms, 4.5 bathrooms, and spans 3,165 square feet.
Built in 1966, the split entry home offers private beach access and is subject to $12,042 in annual taxes.
Readers are invited to guess the home's listing price, with options ranging from $1 million to $1.6 million.

title: 10 best beach reads that should be on your list this summer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston.com compiled a list of 10 beach reads for summer 2026, featuring thrillers, romances, and literary fiction.
Booksellers and readers across New England recommended the titles along with ideal beach spots for reading.
The list aims to enhance summer getaways to locations like Maine's coastline and Cape Cod's sandy shores.

title: Patriots to reportedly open 2026 season with Super Bowl LX rematch vs. Seahawks
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots will open the 2026 season against the Seattle Seahawks in a Super Bowl LX rematch on September 9.
New England lost to Seattle 29-13 in Super Bowl LX, with Patriots QB Drake Maye throwing two interceptions.
The NFL announced the Patriots will play the Detroit Lions in Munich, Germany, on November 15, 2026.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 loss to Nashville SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution's seven-game unbeaten streak ended with a 3-0 loss to Nashville SC at Gillette Stadium.
Nashville SC's Bryan Acosta scored twice, and Warren Madrigal capitalized on a mistake by Revolution's goalkeeper Matt Turner.
With injuries affecting key players, the Revolution's lineup lacked options, impacting their performance ahead of a match with Minnesota United.

title: Massport to attempt Guinness World Record for world’s largest soccer ball
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Massport plans to unveil a 45-foot-tall soccer ball at Piers Park II in East Boston in June.
The current record for the largest soccer ball, set in Doha in 2013, is 38 feet and 11.8 inches.
Massport's display coincides with the FIFA World Cup and includes a Community Day event on June 13.

title: Famous makeup artist gets backlash for mocking former Spirit Airlines employee at Logan over fundraiser
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Makeup artist James Charles criticized a former Spirit Airlines employee seeking funds after losing her job at Logan Airport.
James Charles faced backlash for mocking Amber Lendof Vargas after Spirit Airlines shut down, laying off 17,000 employees.
Charles later donated $5,000 to Vargas's GoFundMe and apologized for his comments, calling them rude and unnecessary.

title: Photos: State trooper killed by wrong-way driver in Lynnfield crash laid to rest
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Trooper Kevin Trainor's funeral was held at St. James Church in Salem, attended by family and colleagues.
Trainor, 30, was killed by a wrong-way driver, Hernan Marrero, 50, on Route 1 in Lynnfield on May 6.
Trainor was a Massachusetts State Trooper living in Georgetown; he grew up in Salem.

title: Angell Animal Medical Center treating cat who fell 10 stories
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mazy, a one-year-old cat, fell nearly ten stories and is receiving treatment at Boston's Angell Animal Medical Center.
The cat sustained broken paws and a collapsed lung, with surgery needed to repair her front legs, MSPCA-Angell reported.
Mazy's treatment might cost up to $15,000, and the MSPCA is seeking donations to cover expenses.

title: AG says auditor can hire attorney to force audit of Legislature after SJC order
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Attorney General Andrea Campbell approved Auditor Diana DiZoglio to hire an attorney to audit the Massachusetts Legislature.
The Supreme Judicial Court set a 30-day deadline for the AG to decide on DiZoglio's request for legal representation.
DiZoglio narrowed her audit's scope to Senate finances for fiscal years 2021 to 2024, following an SJC directive.
